style_prompt:
Mimic the following writing style closely:
- Use short, punchy sentences (averaging under 10 words).
- Tone is casual and informal. Write like you're talking to a friend.
- Use contractions freely (don't, it's, we're, etc.).
- Keep vocabulary simple and everyday.
- Occasionally start sentences with 'And', 'But', or 'So'.
- Use exclamation marks for emphasis and energy.

samples.daily: Had a pretty chill day honestly. Grabbed coffee, knocked out some emails, and then went for a walk. Nothing crazy but it was nice!
samples.explain: Ok so basically a black hole is like this super dense thing in space. It's got so much gravity that nothing can escape it, not even light. That's why it's called a black hole — you literally can't see it.
samples.email_decline: Hey! Thanks for the invite but I can't make it to the meeting on Thursday. Got a conflict I can't move. Let me know if there's notes I can catch up on after!
samples.favorite_place: There's this little coffee shop on the corner near my apartment. It's got these big windows and they always play good music. I just grab my laptop and post up there for hours.
samples.casual_reply: Oh yeah I saw that! Sounds pretty interesting actually. I'm curious what the timeline looks like. Wanna grab lunch and talk about it?
samples.opinion: Honestly I think remote work is way better for most people. You save time on commuting, you can focus better, and you get to be comfortable. But I get that some people miss the social aspect.
samples.freewrite: I've been thinking about learning guitar lately. Like I've wanted to for years but never actually started. Maybe I'll finally just buy a cheap one and watch some YouTube tutorials. Can't be that hard to learn a few chords right?
